variants.name: Baseline (current schole.ai)
variants.thesis: A faithful recreation of Schole's actual landing page: speaks to every audience at once - research pedigree, adoption gap, learner experience, HR outcomes - in one long page.

sections.headline: Faster competency. Higher engagement. Agentic Learning with Scholé.
sections.body: Scholé uses the best of AI to construct exactly the right lesson for each learner. Targeted, practical, and built on 10+ years of AI-for-education research from EPFL and UC Berkeley.

sections.headline: Learn the new way with Scholé
sections.body: Scholé is a multi-agent pedagogical engine for adaptive and personalized learning. No more one-size-fits-all courses.

sections.headline: The adoption gap is real. Scholé closes it.
sections.body: You bought the tools - ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, Claude. But if employees don't know how to use them in their daily work, you're not getting the return. Your employees have different tasks, tools, and backgrounds. They don't all need to learn the same thing, the same way.

sections.headline: Learning that adapts to each person
sections.body: Every employee follows a path built around their tools, skills, and pace.

items.title: Adapts in real time
items.detail: Lesson content adjusts as each learner progresses.

items.title: Tied to daily work
items.detail: Every lesson maps to the learner's actual tools - Notion, Excel, PowerPoint - and job function.

items.title: Measurable outcomes
items.detail: Completion rates, mastery levels, and adoption metrics, trackable in natural language from day one.

sections.headline: What your employees get
sections.body: Ask Olé, an AI tutor team that adjusts to each learner - never too easy, never too hard. Skill scores updated after every lesson, XP and level-ups that reward consistency. 2-minute micro-lessons on mobile, desktop, text, audio, and video.

sections.headline: Teams at these organizations are already learning on Scholé
sections.body: Decathlon Switzerland and the Harvard Data Science Initiative use Scholé today.

items.title: Decathlon
items.detail: "It has significantly improved perceptions of AI at Decathlon Switzerland... The HR dashboard is one of the most business-oriented I have encountered." - Leader of People Development

items.title: Harvard DSI
items.detail: "We work with the Scholé team to teach Harvard Data Science Initiative students online, and there is simply no comparison. They are the best."

sections.headline: Backed by the best, featured in the press
sections.body: Raised $3M with Ace Ventures, The House Fund and Fund F. Recognized by UC Berkeley, EPFL, Harvard, DARPA, and InnoSuisse. #1 at the Learning Engineering Tools Competition. Launched #1 on Product Hunt.

sections.headline: Common questions
sections.body: How is Scholé different from Coursera or LinkedIn Learning? How is it different from ChatGPT? Is it compatible with our existing LMS or HRIS? What does EU AI Act compliance cover?

items.title: LMS/HRIS compatibility
items.detail: Scholé integrates with existing LMS and HRIS systems.

items.title: EU AI Act
items.detail: Scholé helps you prepare for EU AI Act literacy requirements.

sections.headline: Ready to turn AI tools into real AI adoption?
sections.body: Whether you're preparing for the EU AI Act or want your teams to use AI with clarity and confidence, Scholé helps you move from awareness to measurable adoption. 20 minutes, live with a founder.

variants.name: ROI-led
variants.thesis: Bets everything on the HR/L&D buyer who must justify spend: leads with measurable adoption outcomes and CFO-ready reporting, skips learner-experience romance.

sections.headline: Stop reporting completion rates. Start proving AI adoption.
sections.body: Scholé turns AI training into numbers your CFO respects: adoption rates, mastery levels, and time-to-skill - per team, per tool, from day one.

sections.headline: Completion certificates don't survive a budget meeting
sections.body: Most L&D teams can show who finished a course. Almost none can show who changed how they work. That gap is why learning budgets get cut - and why L&D isn't in the room when priorities get set.

sections.headline: The dashboard built for the business, not the LMS admin
sections.body: Ask questions in natural language and get answers about real adoption.

items.title: Adoption, not attendance
items.detail: See which teams actually use AI in their daily tools after training - and which quietly reverted.

items.title: Time-to-skill
items.detail: Track how fast employees reach applied competency, the metric that maps to productivity.

items.title: At-risk alerts
items.detail: "AI adoption in Marketing dropped 6% this quarter" - surfaced automatically, before your QBR.

sections.headline: Why adoption actually moves
sections.body: Scholé's multi-agent engine builds each employee a path around their real tools and job function. When learning maps to Monday morning's actual work, people use it - that's what shows up in your metrics.

sections.headline: "The most business-oriented HR dashboard I have encountered"
sections.body: "A true asset for any L&D leader whose role is increasingly evolving to align more closely with business priorities." - Leader of People Development & Apprenticeship, Decathlon Switzerland

sections.headline: Live in weeks, measurable in the first month
sections.body: Integrates with your LMS and HRIS. No AI specialists required on your team - Scholé's agents do the personalization; your team reads the dashboard.

sections.headline: Bring numbers to your next budget meeting
sections.body: 20 minutes with a founder. We'll show the dashboard on data like yours and leave you with a metric framework you can use either way.

variants.name: Compliance-led
variants.thesis: Bets on regulatory urgency: EU AI Act Article 4 makes AI literacy mandatory, so lead with coverage, auditability, and speed-to-compliant.

sections.headline: EU AI Act Article 4 made AI literacy mandatory. Scholé makes it measurable.
sections.body: Auditable, role-specific AI literacy for your entire workforce - live this quarter, with evidence regulators and boards can read.

sections.headline: What Article 4 actually requires - and how Scholé maps to it
sections.body: Providers and deployers of AI systems must ensure a sufficient level of AI literacy in their staff, accounting for their technical knowledge, experience, and context of use.

items.title: Role-specific literacy
items.detail: Scholé builds each employee's path around their actual role and tools - the 'context of use' the Act requires.

items.title: Audit-ready evidence
items.detail: Per-employee mastery levels and assessment records, exportable for audit - not just completion certificates.

items.title: Kept current
items.detail: As your AI tooling changes, learning paths update - literacy is continuous, not a one-off webinar.

sections.headline: 1,200 employees, one quarter
sections.body: No course authoring, no AI specialists needed. Scholé's agents generate role-mapped curricula from your org structure; your team monitors one dashboard. Typical rollout: weeks, not quarters.

sections.headline: Works with the systems you already run
sections.body: LMS and HRIS integration keeps your records in one place. SSO for painless rollout. Compliance reporting lives alongside your existing people data.

sections.headline: Trusted where the bar is high
sections.body: Decathlon Switzerland runs AI literacy on Scholé. Built on 10+ years of EPFL and UC Berkeley research. Recognized by DARPA and InnoSuisse.

sections.headline: The deadline isn't moving. Neither is your board.
sections.body: 20-minute call: we'll map Article 4 requirements to your org and show you the audit trail live.

variants.name: Problem-first
variants.thesis: Bets on pain recognition: name the adoption gap brutally in the hero, agitate it with the buyer's own data, then present Scholé as the specific fix.

sections.headline: You bought ChatGPT, Copilot, and Claude. Almost nobody uses them.
sections.body: The tools aren't the problem. The training is. Scholé closes the gap between AI licenses you pay for and AI work your teams actually do.

sections.headline: The adoption gap, in your own numbers
sections.body: 83% of HR leaders believe their company supports AI learning. Only 64% of employees agree. Half of your workforce says workload leaves no room for training at all. Meanwhile the licenses renew, and the productivity gains stay theoretical.

sections.headline: Why the last AI training didn't stick
sections.body: One-size-fits-all courses about 'AI in general' don't survive contact with a real calendar. Your analyst, your designer, and your warehouse planner need different skills, in different tools, at different depths. Generic training is ignored because it deserves to be.

sections.headline: The fix: learning built per person, per tool, per job
sections.body: Scholé's multi-agent engine constructs each employee's path around their actual role and daily tools - Notion, Excel, PowerPoint, Slack. 2-minute micro-lessons that fit inside the workday. An AI tutor, Olé, that adjusts to each learner in real time.

sections.headline: And you can watch the gap close
sections.body: Adoption metrics per team and per tool, trackable in natural language. Know which departments moved from 'took the course' to 'changed how they work'.

sections.headline: It's working at Decathlon
sections.body: "It has already significantly improved perceptions of AI at Decathlon Switzerland while helping reshape our learning strategy." - Leader of People Development & Apprenticeship

sections.headline: Your licenses renew either way
sections.body: 20 minutes with a founder. Bring your adoption numbers if you have them - we'll show you what closing the gap looks like.

variants.name: Credibility-led
variants.thesis: Bets on trust in a market full of AI-washing: lead with the research pedigree (EPFL, Berkeley, Harvard, DARPA), position Scholé as science productized.

sections.headline: Ten years of learning-science research. Now a product.
sections.body: Scholé was built by the EPFL Machine Learning for Education lab and UC Berkeley researchers behind 40+ papers on personalized learning. Not another AI wrapper - the actual research, productized.

sections.headline: Recognized by the institutions that set the bar
sections.body: UC Berkeley, EPFL, Harvard, DARPA, and InnoSuisse. #1 at the Learning Engineering Tools Competition - the largest global edtech competition. Forbes recommends Scholé's Harvard co-designed intensives as the #1 way to learn agents in 2026.

items.title: The founders
items.detail: Vinitra Swamy (UC Berkeley, Microsoft AI, EPFL PhD) scaled Berkeley's data science course to 60,000 online learners. Paola Mejia (EPFL PhD) shipped learning tools used by 200,000+ learners.

items.title: The science
items.detail: MultiModN, the modular architecture behind Scholé's agent-first approach, is published, peer-reviewed research.

sections.headline: What a pedagogical engine actually does
sections.body: Scholé models each learner's knowledge state in real time - the same knowledge-tracing research the founders published for a decade - and its agents construct exactly the right next lesson. Never too easy, never too hard, always tied to the learner's real tools.

sections.headline: "There is simply no comparison. They are the best."
sections.body: "AI fluency will set people and companies apart. We work with Vini and her team at Scholé to teach Harvard Data Science Initiative students online." - Harvard Data Science Initiative. Also in production at Decathlon Switzerland.

sections.headline: Research rigor, enterprise fit
sections.body: The science is the differentiator; the deployment is standard enterprise.

sections.headline: Talk to the researchers, not a sales team

variants.name: Learner-first
variants.thesis: Bets on the end user: sell the learner experience (Olé, XP, 2-minute lessons, augmentation-not-automation) and let delighted employees pull the org along. CTA is self-serve.

sections.headline: Learning that fits your workday. Finally.
sections.body: 2-minute lessons built around your actual job and the tools you already use. An AI tutor that meets you exactly where you are. No lecture halls, no 45-minute modules, no generic 'Intro to AI'.

sections.headline: This is about making you faster - not replacing you
sections.body: AI is changing every job, including yours. The people who thrive are the ones who make AI do their boring work. Scholé teaches you to use ChatGPT, Copilot, and Claude on your real tasks, so the skills stay with you - wherever your career goes.

sections.headline: Meet Olé, your tutor team
sections.body: Olé reads your level in real time and responds accordingly - never too easy, never too hard. Not sure where you stand? Ask. Olé reads your learning data and tells you exactly where to focus next.

items.title: You set the dials
items.detail: Rate your prior knowledge, pick difficulty (Fundamental to Expert, with XP multipliers), choose text, video, or interactive.

items.title: Progress you can feel
items.detail: Skill scores after every lesson. XP and level-ups that reward consistency.

items.title: Anywhere
items.detail: Mobile, desktop, audio on your commute, browser extension on the job.

sections.headline: Built on your tools, not textbook examples
sections.body: Lessons map to Notion, Excel, PowerPoint, Slack - and to your job function. You see immediately how each skill applies to the work already on your plate.

sections.headline: Loved by learners
sections.body: Learners at Decathlon and Harvard Data Science Initiative study on Scholé. Launched #1 on Product Hunt, ahead of 400+ products.

sections.headline: Your first lesson takes 2 minutes
sections.body: Start free. Pick your role, pick your tools, and Olé builds your path.

report.insights: Generation 0: v5-learner outperformed the pool with 3.0% conversion (fitness 36.3). Thompson sampling shifted traffic toward it as evidence accumulated. Personas with compliance and ROI objections converted best on focused variants; the baseline page underperformed due to length and diluted messaging. Next generation should preserve winning hero/outcome sections and explicitly resolve the top unresolved objections.

report.insights: Generation 1: g1-demo1 outperformed the pool with 5.7% conversion (fitness 55.9). Thompson sampling shifted traffic toward it as evidence accumulated. Personas with compliance and ROI objections converted best on focused variants; the baseline page underperformed due to length and diluted messaging. Next generation should preserve winning hero/outcome sections and explicitly resolve the top unresolved objections.

report.insights: Generation 2: g2-demo1 outperformed the pool with 3.4% conversion (fitness 37.8). Thompson sampling shifted traffic toward it as evidence accumulated. Personas with compliance and ROI objections converted best on focused variants; the baseline page underperformed due to length and diluted messaging. Next generation should preserve winning hero/outcome sections and explicitly resolve the top unresolved objections.

Unresolved roi_proof remains the top conversion blocker.
